details.body:
finding_summary: Failure reconciliation is still dead code at runtime, so failed bead tasks never update milestone lineage/controller state.
affected_files: src/contexts/automation_runtime/mod.rs, src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs, src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs
files_and_lines: src/contexts/automation_runtime/mod.rs:8,25-28 only exports the new handler; src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1103-1105 and 1732-1734 still invoke only `try_reconcile_success`, while actual run failures go straight to `DaemonTaskService::mark_failed` at src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1177-1188 without any failure reconciliation.
remediation_hint: add a failure-side reconciliation entry point in the daemon, parallel to `try_reconcile_success`, and invoke it before marking the daemon task failed so `BeadFailed`, retry counts, and `NeedsOperator` escalation are actually recorded.

finding_summary: Replaying a failed run after a partial milestone-state write will not repair missing journal/snapshot state.
affected_files: src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs, src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs
files_and_lines: src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:96-132 treats any existing failed lineage row as `already_recorded` and skips `record_bead_completion`; src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2633-2684 shows that lineage is updated before snapshot/journal commit, so a crash can leave the failed task-run row durable while the `BeadFailed` event / failed counter never land; src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:545-603 already uses `repair_task_run_with_disposition` to recover this exact class of replay.
remediation_hint: mirror the success path here: detect an already-terminal run and route through `repair_task_run_with_disposition` (or equivalent) so replay repairs missing `BeadFailed` journal/snapshot state instead of treating the run as a no-op.

finding_summary: Duplicate failure callbacks are not idempotent once a retry has started; they can clobber or reject the active retry's controller state.
affected_files: src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs, src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs, src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs
files_and_lines: src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:341-398 only short-circuits replays when the controller is already in the desired failed state; otherwise it always re-enters `sync_controller_task_reconciling` and transitions again. `retry_task` reuses the same daemon task id at src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:485-515, and `sync_controller_task_reconciling`/`validate_active_context_alignment` at src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:545-590 and 1469-1498 will either push a currently running retry back to `Reconciling`/`Blocked` or reject the replay if a newer task owns the bead.
remediation_hint: make the replay guard key off the reconciled run identity and/or detect a newer nonterminal attempt in lineage before mutating controller state.

finding_summary: `MAX_FAILURE_RETRIES` will not trigger for ordinary task retries, because the retry path reuses the same workflow `run_id` and the lineage layer reopens the old failed row in place. `failure_attempt_number()` treats an existing `(project_id, run_id)` failure as already recorded and reuses its original ordinal (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:320-331`), failed daemon tasks are resumed via `resume_run_with_retry_and_capture_run_id` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:4267-4285`), and resume keeps the prior run id (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:7029-7030`). On start, `record_task_run_start()` calls `reopen_failed_exact_attempt()` for that same run id instead of appending a new attempt row (`src/adapters/fs.rs:4546-4571`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:5014-5030`). The result is that repeated resume failures keep a single failed lineage entry, so attempt 2/3 are never observed and escalation to `NeedsOperator` never happens. remediation_hint: either persist a distinct lineage attempt for each resume of a failed run, or derive retry counts from durable failure/resume cycles rather than unique run ids.

finding_summary: Retryable failures lose the identity of the bead that is supposed to be retried. On the retryable branch, `reconcile_failure()` transitions to `Blocked` without preserving `bead_id` / `task_id` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:383-394`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:484-490`). `Blocked` is one of the controller states that explicitly clears active context (`src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:1441-1447`), and blocked resumes are then treated as generic selection state that only asks whether any ready bead exists (`src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:1210-1219`). `milestone next/run` also immediately re-enters bead selection whenever the controller is `Blocked` (`src/cli/milestone.rs:648-656`). That means the new "retry remains available" state is not actionable for the failed bead itself: the system either stalls as a generic blocked milestone or advances to some other ready bead, instead of preserving targeted retry state for the failed bead.

finding_summary: Failure reconciliation depends on a durable `RunFailed`/`RunStarted` pair in the project journal and aborts otherwise (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1903-1912`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1925-1939`), but the workflow engine explicitly treats `run_failed` journal persistence as best-effort and ignores append failures (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs:6165-6177`). In that real partial-write scenario the daemon still marks the task failed, but `try_reconcile_failure()` returns `reconciliation_metadata_error` and never records the milestone-side `BeadFailed` event or retry/escalation state. remediation_hint: fall back to snapshot/interrupted-run metadata or existing lineage start data when the project journal is incomplete, instead of hard-failing the whole reconciliation path.

finding_summary: Failure reconciliation stores the daemon task ID in `controller.active_task_id`, but the controller runtime consistently interprets that field as the bead-backed project ID. The new daemon wiring passes `&task.task_id` into `reconcile_failure` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2047-2055`), and `transition_controller_after_failure` writes that value back into the blocked/needs-operator controller transitions (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:481-498`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:545-552`). Both milestone resume paths then parse `active_task_id` with `ProjectId::new(...)` and require that project to exist (`src/cli/milestone.rs:810-819`, `src/cli/milestone.rs:1252-1264`, `src/cli/run.rs:989-1000`). After a daemon-managed failure, the controller now points at the daemon task record instead of the project, so `milestone run`/resume cannot reopen the failed bead and falls into missing/invalid-project errors instead of retrying.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs
remediation_hint: Keep recording the daemon task ID in journal/outcome detail, but use `project_id` for controller transitions so blocked/retry resume continues to target the existing project.

finding_summary: The new blocked-retry resume branch requests a direct `Blocked -> Running` controller transition, but that edge is still illegal in the controller state machine. `resume_transition_request` now returns `MilestoneControllerState::Running` when a blocked retry task is already running (`src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:1227-1240`), while `MilestoneControllerState::allows_transition_to` still only allows `Blocked -> Selecting|Claimed|Completed|NeedsOperator` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs:27-55`). On any restart while a retried project is already running, `resume_controller` will therefore reject the state change as an illegal transition instead of adopting the in-flight retry.
affected_files:
- src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs
remediation_hint: Either route that resume case back through an allowed intermediate state such as `Claimed`, or explicitly add/support `Blocked -> Running` everywhere the journal/state validators expect legal transitions, with coverage for restart-during-retry.

finding_summary: The manual milestone retry path that this patch exposes does not actually use the new attempt-aware failure reconciliation logic. `execute_milestone_run` now treats blocked retry context as executable and resumes the existing project (`src/cli/milestone.rs:720-756`), but the terminal sync path it calls on failure still uses the old generic behavior: failed runs go through `sync_controller_task_claimed(...)` plus `record_bead_completion_with_disposition(...)` (`src/cli/run.rs:1336-1384`) rather than `reconcile_failure`. Because `execute_milestone_run` then maps any failed project snapshot straight to `MilestoneCommandStatus::NeedsOperator` (`src/cli/milestone.rs:737-756`), second/third failures triggered from `milestone run` are never evaluated against `MAX_FAILURE_RETRIES`; they bypass the new blocked-vs-needs-operator policy entirely.
affected_files:
- src/cli/milestone.rs
- src/cli/run.rs
remediation_hint: Route failed `run start`/`run resume` terminal sync through the same failure-reconciliation helper (or a shared attempt-aware helper) and derive the CLI result from the post-sync controller outcome instead of the raw failed project snapshot.

The `allows_transition_to` method in `src/contexts/milestone_record/controller.rs` (lines 52-55) is missing `(State::Blocked, State::Running)` and `(State::Blocked, State::Reconciling)` edges. The new `resume_transition_request` logic (line ~1229) for Blocked-with-retry-context generates these transitions when: (a) the retry task is already Running, or (b) the retry task already Succeeded. But `transition_from_current_locked` (line 966) enforces `allows_transition_to`, so these resume transitions would fail with a 'corrupt controller' error. Fix: add `| (State::Blocked, State::Running) | (State::Blocked, State::Reconciling)` to the match arms in `allows_transition_to`.

finding_summary: Failure reconciliation guesses the current run from the latest journal metadata when `workflow_run_id` is missing, so a retry/manual rerun can be reconciled against the wrong historical attempt.
severity: high
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1913-1926
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1997-2006
remediation_hint: Mirror the success path and require a durable task-to-run binding, or at minimum an exact active/interrupted snapshot attempt, before reconciling. Do not infer `run_id`/`started_at` from the latest journal entry.

finding_summary: When daemon-side failure reconciliation cannot determine milestone metadata, the task is marked failed but the milestone controller is left in its prior active state instead of being pushed to `NeedsOperator`.
severity: medium
affected_files:
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1183-1201
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1781-1799
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1866-1931
- src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2001-2008
remediation_hint: For milestone-backed tasks, convert hard reconciliation metadata failures into a controller `NeedsOperator` transition before returning the task failure, matching the existing success-reconciliation behavior.
